overview.short: Korigad (also called Koraigad, Koarigad or Kumwarigad) is a hill fort located about 18 km (11 mi) south of Lonavala in Pune district, Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Korigad (also called Koraigad, Koarigad or Kumwarigad) is a hill fort located about 18 km (11 mi) south of Lonavala in Pune district, Maharashtra, India. Its date of construction is not known but likely predates 1500. It is about 923 m above sea level. The planned township of Aamby Valley is built over the fort's southern and eastern foothills.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Korigad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Korigad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (56 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (52 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Korigad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Korigad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Korigad?
faq.a: Stays in Korigad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Korigad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Aamby Valley City, Shilimb, Chavsar, Kadav, Deoghar, Pune.
